goal: Planner produces a plan artifact and hands off to Orchestrator via plan_path — Orchestrator receives it and enters PLAN_REVIEW or skips based on tier

inputs.label: medium-plan-handoff
inputs.description: Planner receives a medium-complexity request, produces a plan artifact, and hands off to Orchestrator. Orchestrator enters PLAN_REVIEW.

input.query: Refactor the authentication module to use JWT tokens across 8 files
input.context: Medium complexity: cross-domain refactor touching auth, API, and config files. Planner should produce plan and delegate to Orchestrator.

inputs.label: trivial-plan-handoff
inputs.description: Planner receives a trivial request, produces a minimal plan, and hands off to Orchestrator. Orchestrator skips PLAN_REVIEW.

input.query: Fix the typo in README.md line 42
input.context: Trivial: single file, single concern. Planner should produce plan and delegate to Orchestrator which skips PLAN_REVIEW.

assertions: Planner always creates a plan file and emits plan_path for Orchestrator
Planner handoff message includes complexity_tier so Orchestrator can route correctly
Planner handoff remains artifact-first and chat stays a concise summary instead of inlining the plan body
Orchestrator does not treat plan_path handoff as implicit approval — it evaluates PLAN_REVIEW trigger conditions
TRIVIAL plans skip PLAN_REVIEW per runtime-policy.json; MEDIUM plans enter full PLAN_REVIEW
Semantic-risk trigger heuristics remain anchored to plans/project-context.md even when Planner emits only the saved artifact and handoff summary
